title: Measure cytokine and anti-TREM2 effects on myeloid, NK and T cell activity
text: The objective of this research task (1.1) is to evaluate the synergistic effects of pro-inflammatory cytokines in conjunction with the E3C7 monoclonal antibody (mAb) targeting TREM2 on the activation of myeloid, natural killer (NK), and T cells. This will be achieved through a series of in-vitro assays involving co-cultured human monocyte-derived macrophages (huMDMs), NK cells, and T cells. The experimental design includes control groups where assays will be conducted without the anti-TREM2 mAb and without the addition of cytokines, allowing for a comparative analysis of immune cell activity. The primary data collection methods will involve flow cytometry to quantify the proliferative potential of NK and T cells, alongside enzyme-linked immunosorbent assays (ELISA) and quantitative polymerase chain reaction (qPCR) to measure cytokine secretion levels. The results will be analyzed using statistical methods appropriate for flow cytometry data, such as paired t-tests or ANOVA, to determine the significance of differences between experimental and control groups. Additionally, cytokine secretion data will be interpreted in the context of immune cell activation profiles, providing insights into the mechanistic pathways influenced by the cytokine and mAb combinations. This task is designed to be completed within a defined timeframe, ensuring that the findings contribute to the broader objectives of identifying optimal anti-TREM2 MiTE candidates and understanding their immune-modulating effects, as outlined in the overarching research aims. The anticipated outcomes will not only elucidate the role of TREM2 in immune modulation but also inform subsequent phases of the project focused on the development and validation of lead MiTE molecules for therapeutic applications.
